claim: NIST defines AI as a machine-based system that makes predictions, recommendations, or decisions for human-defined objectives.
quote: A machine-based system that can, for a given set of human-defined objectives, make predictions, recommendations, or decisions influencing real or virtual environments.

claim: AI, ML, deep learning and neural networks form a nested hierarchy — each a subfield of the one before.
quote: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning, and machine learning is a subset of AI.

claim: An LLM is a neural network trained on vast text for natural-language tasks, typically built on the transformer architecture.
quote: A large language model (LLM) is a neural network trained on a vast amount of text for natural language processing tasks, especially language generation.

claim: LLMs are trained by iteratively adjusting parameters to predict the next token from previous tokens.
quote: Iteratively adjust parameter values until the model correctly predicts the next token from an the previous sequence of input tokens.

claim: Google describes LLMs as autocomplete mechanisms that predict tokens, with self-attention deciding how each token influences the others.
quote: Autocomplete mechanisms that can automatically predict (complete) thousands of tokens.

claim: A foundation model is one trained on broad data at scale that can be adapted to many downstream tasks — the term was coined by Stanford CRFM in 2021.
quote: Any model that is trained on broad data (generally using self-supervision at scale) that can be adapted (e.g., fine-tuned) to a wide range of downstream tasks.

claim: Stanford's Center for Research on Foundation Models is the academic group that coined and studies foundation models.
quote: The Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM) is an interdisciplinary initiative born out of the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI).

claim: A generative model learns the joint distribution P(D,L) and can produce new data; a discriminative model learns P(D|L) and classifies.
quote: A generative model learns the joint probability distribution P(D, L)... a discriminative model learns the conditional probability distribution P(D | L).

claim: RAG optimises an LLM's output by referencing an authoritative knowledge base outside its training data before generating a response.
quote: The process of optimizing the output of a large language model, so it references an authoritative knowledge base outside of its training data sources before generating a response.

claim: AI agents (also called agentic AI) are intelligent systems that operate autonomously in complex environments and prioritise decisions over content.
quote: AI agents (also referred to as compound AI systems or agentic AI) are a class of intelligent agents distinguished by their ability to operate autonomously in complex environments.

claim: An AI hallucination is a response that contains false or misleading information presented as fact.
quote: A response generated by AI that contains false or misleading information presented as fact.

claim: Models hallucinate partly because training and evaluation reward confident guessing over admitting uncertainty.
quote: Language models are optimized to be good test-takers, and guessing when uncertain improves test performance.

claim: Prompt injection — ranked #1 in the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 — occurs when user prompts alter the LLM's behaviour in unintended ways, in either direct or indirect form.
quote: A Prompt Injection Vulnerability occurs when user prompts alter the LLM's behavior or output in unintended ways.

claim: RLHF uses reinforcement learning to optimise a language model against human preference rankings, aligning it with values that are hard to write as a loss function.
quote: Use methods from reinforcement learning to directly optimize a language model with human feedback. RLHF has enabled language models to begin to align a model trained on a general corpus of text data to that of complex human values.

claim: A token is the smallest unit of text an LLM processes; the context window is how many tokens the model can hold in view at once.
quote: Tokens are the basic building blocks for LLMs and represent the smallest unit of text the model can understand and process.

claim: By 2026, production LLM context windows range from ~128K tokens (DeepSeek, Mistral) up to 10 million (Llama 4 Scout).
quote: Context windows range from 128K tokens (DeepSeek, Mistral) to 10 million (Llama 4 Scout).

claim: LLM training has two distinct phases: pretraining on raw text via next-token prediction, followed by alignment (supervised fine-tuning + RLHF).
quote: The training objective is simple: predict the next token given all preceding tokens.
